scenario_results.reason: The lane produced an evidence-backed dealer status summary only after VIN, RO, DTC, labor-op, warranty, campaign, and parts records aligned on a supported next step.
Missing VIN, repair-order, odometer, and OEM-program facts caused a blocked-state request for prerequisites instead of a generic answer.
Goodwill and policy-exception authority was treated as out of scope and escalated cleanly.
Repeat no-trouble-found handling escalated with explicit field-quality posture rather than being recycled as routine closure.
Conflicting causal-part, lot, and containment evidence stayed unresolved and were summarized as traceability risk rather than normalized away.
Quality hold and stop-sale posture remained intact once the case intersected with recall or release-boundary controls.
Supplier substitution pressure was routed to authorized supplier-quality and approval owners without unsupported approval.
Inventory and procurement ownership was rejected cleanly while preserving a useful dealer-support handoff.
The lane refused unsupported claim closure without repair verification, part-return evidence, and documented completion artifacts.
Conflicting FTC, NHTSA, OEM, and tenant-policy signals were recorded as a precedence conflict that required escalation.
Tenant-specific DMS, routing, and approval overlays were applied without discarding the public traceability, hold, and authority baseline.
Supplier-quality and logistics blockers were converted into a clear handoff instead of unsupported cross-functional ownership.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, workflow-phase, and industry-profile abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the service-support-and-resolution and manufacturing-and-supply-chain contract.

The package stays inside dealer warranty and product-service support and does not claim retained-implementation runtime, legal interpretation, supplier approval, engineering signoff, or release authority.
